title: RIOT Quarterly Earnings Surprises and Beat/Miss Patterns 2024-2026
text: RIOT Platforms has demonstrated mixed earnings surprise patterns across recent quarters, with revenue generally outperforming consensus expectations while earnings per share results have been more volatile. In Q4 2025, the company produced earnings of $0.26 per share versus consensus estimates of a loss of $0.19 per share, delivering a positive surprise of +236.84%. In Q1 2026, the company reported revenues of $167 million against consensus forecasts of $122.26 million, representing a revenue beat of 26.45%. However, the company reported a net loss of $1.44 per share, significantly worse than the consensus estimate of a loss of $0.33 per share. Despite the significant EPS miss in Q1 2026, the market reaction was overwhelmingly positive, with shares rising 7.7% during regular trading and an additional 2% in aftermarket trading. This positive reaction reflected the strategic importance of the revenue beat and the inflection point in the company's data center business transition. In Q4 2024, RIOT generated $376.7 million in full-year revenue, beating estimates by 1.1%, with earnings per share surpassing forecasts by 111%. The pattern suggests that investors are increasingly focused on revenue growth and strategic business model transition rather than near-term earnings per share metrics, particularly given the company's transition from pure commodity mining to higher-margin data center infrastructure operations.
